replies.text: ​@ludivicolabs Agoraphobia is a complex anxiety disorder characterized by an intense fear of, and avoidance of, places or situations that might cause panic, helplessness, or embarrassment. author: @SetsunaMeiou-SailorPluto
text:
One thing I haven't seen anyone mention yet is that if it is filming all of this it's also filming your kids. When you change your baby's diaper. Give them a bath. Help them change their clothes. Take them to daycare. Take them to school. It's filming your children at all times and people could easily take that and sell those photos and videos of your children or sell their location info and take them and you wouldn't be any the wiser.

Someone could even see that you're single and live alone and exactly where you are located and your daily routine with these glasses. Where you work. What your exercise routine is like. Where you jog. Where you walk your dog. Places you frequent. You are broadcasting all of this about yourself at all times to any number of unknown individuals with unknown intentions that could do you harm.

It doesn't even have to be people in another country when we have data breach notifications daily.

These need to be banned entirely.

author: @witchyfarmgirl
text:
Back in  April 2020 in SE Minnesota a young woman was killed by her husband. She was reported missing & a huge search w/ hundreds of volunteers walked fields & ditches. 

My brother had the Ring Camera. His next door neighbor is part of the Sheriffs Office. He told my Brother back then that they were using his Ring Camera Footage to find the vehicle of the suspect. My brother didn’t even “Allow/Consent” for Ring Camera footage to be used. His neighbor, the Deputy, informed my brother that he had no rights. 

The husband of the missing woman was found guilty of murder. They used the Ring Camera Footage at the trial. My brother stopped using the Ring Camera after that incident. Almost 6 yrs later they finally told everyone in a Super-Bowl Ad what they were doing all along. 🤦🏼‍♀️😑🤦🏼‍♀️😑🤦🏼‍♀️

replies.author: @deardra5619
replies.text:
@Karamarika I don’t know if the news reports was correct it was about 10 years ago. However, the seers do say that all devices will have to leave if one is to survive ww3 and the aftermath. I know they are listening to your every word. Your every call. I was a senior systems requirements analyst for a huge telecommunications company for 18 years. They can even watch on your phone anytime any place so if you don’t cover, they can be watching. So based on what I know about telecommunications I am about 95% certain most tv can spy on you. 
The ridiculous part is we pay the internet fee for them to spy on us…..UGH!

author: @BazH-p3p
text: I think the bigger issue is why people are wearing glasses that they know are actively recording while they are in the bathroom, banking, having sex, undressing, etc? It's common sense and it's not like they don't know the glasses are recording.  The simple solution is to take them off if you're doing something you don't want to be recorded. Not to mention, if you are not aware that they are recording, you're a fool to assume Facebook doesn't have them set to always record by default.

replies.author: @kciwner
replies.text: They are prescription glasses. Taking them off would make a lot of people as good as blind.

replies.author: @BazH-p3p
replies.text: ​@kciwnerThat makes sense, but with knowing the history of Facebook, AI, and the value of our data, if you can't see without glasses, I think it would be a better choice to just choose regular glasses that don't have the ability to record. We've already learned we can't give these tech companies the benefit of the doubt.

Well, I can tell you, they most likely called whoever in their lives that tried to tell them a "conspiracy theorist ", a kook, a crazy schizoid, or something else along those lines.
